name: nav-editorial-section
role: Magazine / editorial-style top navigation. Distinct from marketing nav (heavily CTA-driven, dropdown-rich) and docs nav (sidebar tree); the editorial convention foregrounds the masthead, presents categories as a strong horizontal rule, and keeps utility actions (sign in, subscribe) in a clearly-separated row. Three registers — stacked (masthead row + nav row + utility row, the canonical newspaper layout), single-row (everything compressed into one line, for narrow viewports or condensed-aesthetic publications), centered-masthead (logo center-anchored, categories distributed left-and-right around it).

semanticStructure: <nav-editorial-utility> — top utility row (sign in, subscribe, search, date)

registers.stacked: Three rows top-to-bottom: utility row (small, top) → masthead (large, centered) → categories (medium, below). The classic newspaper masthead layout. Used by NYT, The Guardian, FT, Atlantic.
registers.single-row: Everything compressed: small logo + categories + utility in one horizontal row. Used at narrow viewports OR for condensed publications.
registers.centered-masthead: Wordmark centered, categories distributed left and right around it. Used by editorial brands with strong central-mark identity (some legacy newspaper styles).

weights.heavy: Border-width-md dividers (thicker), type-size-xl masthead. Newspaper-classic feel.

ariaPatterns.navLandmark: <nav aria-label='Sections'> wraps the categories list. Distinguishes from utility nav.
ariaPatterns.utilityRole: Utility row uses <div role='group' aria-label='Account and search'> for the cluster of utility actions, OR a separate <nav aria-label='Utility'> if it contains primary nav links.

name: nav-editorial-masthead
role: Wordmark / brand row. Renders as <div> containing the wordmark <a> + optional edition date. Wordmark uses --font-display at type-size-2xl by default (oversized for editorial impact). Edition date is small uppercase tracking-wide. Centered in stacked register; left-anchored in single-row.

meta.wordmarkSemantics: The wordmark is wrapped in <a href='/' aria-label='...'> — the masthead is the primary link to home. Don't wrap it in <h1> — the editorial page's <h1> belongs to the article headline, not the brand name.
meta.editionDateOptional: Edition date adds editorial gravitas. Newspapers traditionally show 'Saturday, December 14, 2026'. Web publications might show 'Issue 247 · December 2026'.

name: nav-editorial-categories
role: Horizontal list of section category links. The editorial equivalent of nav-marketing's primary nav, but visually heavier and with strong active-category indicator. Renders as <ul> inside <nav aria-label='Sections'>. Each <li><a> is a category. Active category gets a heavy underline indicator + emphasized weight.

ariaPatterns.currentPageMarker: Active category link gets aria-current='page'. Visible 3px underline indicator in --accent + emphasized weight.
ariaPatterns.uppercaseTreatment: uppercase='true' applies text-transform: uppercase + tracking-wide. Classic newspaper section-heading aesthetic. Set uppercase='false' for sentence-case treatment (some modern editorial brands prefer this).

meta.spacingRationale: dense for ≥8 categories; default for 5-7; spaced for 3-4 categories. Wide spacing makes few categories feel deliberate; tight spacing makes many categories scannable.

name: nav-editorial-utility
role: Top utility row containing account actions (sign in / account avatar), subscribe pill (composes button-system), inline search trigger, optional edition date or weather/locale info. Small typography. Sits ABOVE the masthead in stacked register; inline with masthead in single-row.

registers.standard: Sign in link + subscribe pill + search trigger. The 80% choice.
registers.extended: Adds edition date + weather/locale + saved-articles count. Used by full-feature editorial brands.

ariaPatterns.groupRole: <div role='group' aria-label='Utility actions'> wraps the cluster.
ariaPatterns.subscribePillAccentIntent: Subscribe button uses intent='primary' for visual priority — it's the editorial brand's primary conversion.

name: nav-editorial-search-inline
role: Inline expandable search. Initially renders as just a magnifier icon button; on click, expands into a horizontal input field with submit button. Distinct from nav-marketing's Cmd+K palette — editorial convention favors visible inline search over keyboard-shortcut palette.

semanticStructure: (when expanded) <input id='editorial-search-input' type='search' name='q' placeholder='Search articles…' />
meta.expandBehaviour: Collapsed: just the icon button. On click, input animates from width 0 to 240px (200ms ease-standard). Input gets autofocus when expanded.

ariaPatterns.searchRole: <form role='search'> identifies this as the search landmark.
ariaPatterns.expandToggle: Icon button has aria-expanded + aria-controls pointing to the input's id. State-paired aria-label ('Expand search' / 'Close search').
ariaPatterns.labelRequired: Input must have a <label> (visually hidden via class='sr-only' is fine) — never aria-label only on the input itself if a visible label can be provided.

name: nav-editorial-subscribe-cta
role: Subscribe pill — the primary conversion CTA in the editorial nav. Composes <action-button intent='primary' size='sm'> from pattern-button-system, styled as a pill (full border-radius). Often the only prominently-colored element in the entire nav.

registers.outline: Transparent ground with --accent border + text. Used in heavier-weight publications where the pill ground would compete with the masthead.

meta.composesFrom: @quoin/pattern-button-system (visually equivalent to action-button intent='primary' size='sm' with radius=pill override)
meta.copyDiscipline: Label is imperative verb-first: 'Subscribe' / 'Subscribe now' / 'Get the newsletter'. Not 'Click here' / 'Sign up'.

name: nav-editorial-mobile-toggle
role: Hamburger button for mobile drawer. Toggles full-screen overlay containing the categories list, utility actions, and subscribe pill. Same WAI-ARIA pattern as nav-marketing's nav-mobile-toggle: aria-expanded + aria-controls + state-paired aria-label.

meta.ariaLabelStatePair: aria-label switches between 'Open menu' (closed) and 'Close menu' (open).
meta.visibilityBreakpoint: Hidden at min-width:961px. Visible at max-width:960px.
meta.drawerContents: The mobile drawer contains: subscribe pill (at top), categories list (vertically stacked), utility actions, search expanded inline.
meta.focusManagement: On open, focus moves to first focusable inside drawer. On close, focus returns to the toggle. Escape closes.
